criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/lower.sh defines a function lower that prints its first argument with ASCII A-Z mapped to a-z.
criteria.evidence: Diff scripts/lib/lower.sh lines 3-5: `lower() { printf '%s\n' "${1-}" | LC_ALL=C tr 'A-Z' 'a-z'; }` — LC_ALL=C tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' maps exactly ASCII uppercase to lowercase and prints the result.
tests/lower.sh line 20: `assert_lower 'ABC' 'abc'` compares $(lower "$input") to expected; orchestrator evidence: `bash tests/lower.sh exited 0` under set -euo pipefail, so this assertion passed.
tests/lower.sh line 21: `assert_lower 'AbC1' 'abc1'`; tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' leaves '1' untouched; test exited 0 per orchestrator evidence.
tests/lower.sh line 22: `assert_lower '' ''`; `printf '%s\n' "" | tr ...` yields only a newline, stripped by command substitution to the empty string; test exited 0 per orchestrator evidence.

criteria.criterion: tests/lower.sh sources the helper and asserts those cases; it exits 0 when all assertions pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/lower.sh line 5: `source scripts/lib/lower.sh` (after cd to repo root, line 3); lines 20-22 assert all three specified cases; `set -euo pipefail` (line 2) makes any assert_lower failure (return 1, lines 13-17) abort non-zero. Orchestrator: `bash tests/lower.sh exited 0`.

scope_finding: Diff touches only scripts/lib/lower.sh (matches in_scope glob scripts/lib/**) and tests/lower.sh (explicitly in_scope). No out-of-scope paths (.github/**, specs/**, .orchestrator/**) touched. Orchestrator scope check: PASS. No drift.
regression_finding: None observed — both files are new additions (diff shows `new file mode 100755` for each); no existing code, tests, or configuration is modified or deleted, and the helper defines only the single function `lower`, so no existing behaviour can be affected.
security_findings: None observed — no secrets or credentials in the diff, no network access (consistent with needs_network: false), and shell usage is safe: arguments are quoted (`\"${1-}\"`, `\"$input\"`), printf format strings are literal, and the only external command is `tr` with fixed literal arguments under LC_ALL=C.
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are MET with direct evidence from the diff plus the orchestrator's passing test run (bash tests/lower.sh exited 0).

The lower function uses LC_ALL=C tr 'A-Z' 'a-z', which correctly maps only ASCII letters, leaves non-letters unchanged, and handles the empty string; "${1-}" also keeps it safe under set -u with zero arguments.

tests/lower.sh sources the helper from the repo root and asserts exactly the three specified cases; set -euo pipefail guarantees a non-zero exit if any assertion fails, so the observed exit 0 confirms all assertions passed.

Changed paths are entirely within the approved scope, the change is purely additive with no regression surface, and no security concerns (secrets, unsafe shell, credential access, network use) are present.
